idea.hook: Road trip to a star? Prepare for a LONG commute!
facts: Driving to Proxima Centauri (our nearest star) at highway speed (70 mph) would take over 40 MILLION years.
At the speed of our fastest spacecraft (New Horizons, ~36,000 mph), it would still take around 79,000 years.
Only at light speed (the cosmic limit) would the journey to Proxima Centauri take a 'mere' 4.24 years.
idea.payoff: Makes you wonder if we'll ever meet alien neighbors... or if they've already given up waiting!
idea.title: Cosmic Commute: How Long to Reach Another Star? 🚀
idea.description: Ever dreamed of visiting another star? ✨ Our nearest neighbor, Proxima Centauri, seems close but the travel time will utterly blow your mind! From highway speeds to light speed, prepare to have your perspective on interstellar travel completely reshaped. Will humanity ever make the journey?

scenes.visual: A stylized Earth in the foreground with a faint, shimmering arrow pointing towards a single, bright distant star (Proxima Centauri) against a deep space background.
A cartoon car drives slowly along a dotted 'road' through space, Earth shrinking behind it, Proxima Centauri still incredibly far ahead. A digital clock ticks rapidly into the millions.

scenes.text: Our fastest spacecraft (36,000 mph)? Still 79,000 YEARS!
scenes.visual: A sleek, fast-moving spacecraft (like New Horizons) streaks across the screen from left to right, passing by gas giants. The distant star appears slightly closer, but still very far. Timer shows thousands of years.
Streaks of light and a tunnel-like effect simulate warp speed, rapidly approaching Proxima Centauri, which grows larger and brighter. A digital counter quickly counts down to 4.24.

scenes.text: Will we ever meet alien neighbors... or have they given up waiting?
scenes.visual: A swirling, mysterious nebula with a faint, ambiguous silhouette (could be a head/eyes) emerging and then fading into the cosmic dust, leaving a sense of wonder and vastness.
